text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers merge their code changes into a shared repository several times a day. Each time code is added, an automated build is triggered. This process compiles the code and runs a suite of tests to check for errors. By doing this, teams can catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. The entire workflow, including building and testing, is managed through a CI pipeline. This pipeline ensures that every change is verified quickly, helping the team maintain a stable and reliable codebase throughout development.
